$comment: n8n-MCP update_workflow operations for the LinkedInScaler workflow. Replace <YOUR_*> placeholders with your own values, then call update_workflow(workflowId, operations). Atomic and idempotent. The Generate flow needs only the OpenRouter credential; the Publish flow additionally needs a LinkedIn credential backed by your own LinkedIn Developer app.

placeholders.<YOUR_OPENROUTER_CRED_ID>: id of your openRouterApi credential (from list_credentials)
placeholders.<YOUR_LINKEDIN_CRED_ID>: id of your linkedInOAuth2Api credential (Publish flow only)
placeholders.<YOUR_MEMBER_URN>: your LinkedIn author URN, e.g. urn:li:person:XXXXXXXX (Publish flow only)

notes: The two flows are independent webhooks. The Generate flow (POST /webhook/linkedinscaler-generate) runs with just the OpenRouter credential and returns a ready-to-post LinkedIn post as text.

The Publish flow (POST /webhook/linkedinscaler-publish) requires a LinkedIn Developer app (https://www.linkedin.com/developers/apps/new) with the w_member_social and openid profile scopes, plus an authorised linkedInOAuth2Api credential. Until you have that, the template's 'LinkedIn - Create Post' node is intentionally left with an empty author URN and no credential.
update_workflow is atomic — apply each array in its own call, or merge them, but a single failed op rolls back the whole batch.
